acceptance_criteria: Phase 2 has clear child tasks, GSD phase docs reference the active work, and the phase closes only after verification passes across the self-hosted workflow.
close_reason: Verified: Phase 2 doctrine, cleanup, doctor, adoption fixtures, and dogfood validation completed and documented in .planning/phases/phase-2-self-hosted-harness-rollout/
description: Finish CORE-05 by making ai-harness the opinionated Beads + GSD + Codex/OpenCode foundation for new and existing repos. This phase should lock the product doctrine, harden curated cleanup for non-harness AI workflow droppings, strengthen adoption and doctor coverage, and validate the dogfood loop on this repository plus sample repos.
title: Phase 2: self-hosted harness rollout

acceptance_criteria: The doctrine clearly states what ai-harness installs, what it removes, what it preserves, and which files are the source of truth for dogfooded updates.
close_reason: Verified: doctrine now defines the installed foundation, preserve/remove contract, and source-of-truth rules in README.md, docs/ai-harness-premise.md, and .planning/PROJECT.md
description: Write down the opinionated product doctrine for ai-harness: Beads, GSD, Codex, and OpenCode are the foundation; src/templates/** is canonical; existing repos preserve user files by default; and non-harness AI workflow droppings are only removed through curated manifests. Update the project and planning docs so later cleanup and doctor work has a stable contract to follow.
title: Codify ai-harness doctrine and cleanup contract

acceptance_criteria: Curated manifest entries cover the known legacy droppings we want removed, and tests prove they are removed only when explicitly requested.
close_reason: Verified: legacy-ai-frameworks-v1 now covers deprecated .planning/TRACEABILITY.md and cleanup behavior passes unit, integration, typecheck, full test, and dist smoke validation
description: Inventory the non-harness AI workflow leftovers we already know how to reason about, then extend curated cleanup manifests so existing-repo adoption can remove them safely. Keep the scope explicit: legacy wrappers, duplicate planning systems, deprecated assistant runtime files, and other known artifacts that conflict with the ai-harness foundation.
title: Expand curated cleanup manifests for legacy AI workflow droppings

acceptance_criteria: The test matrix covers representative clean, legacy, and mixed states so future adoption changes can be made without guessing.
close_reason: Verified: mixed-state adoption coverage now covers clean, legacy, and mixed existing-repo states and passes targeted tests, full test suite, typecheck, and dist smoke validation
description: Add integration coverage for the adoption states we care about most: clean repos, repos with older ai-harness/scaiff artifacts, repos with mixed custom AI files, and repos with conflicting workflow leftovers. Use these fixtures to prove preserve-by-default behavior and curated cleanup behavior together.
title: Build adoption fixture matrix for mixed repository states

acceptance_criteria: Doctor reports deprecated known artifacts with actionable guidance without encouraging broad heuristic deletion of user files.
close_reason: Verified: doctor now flags known curated deprecated artifacts with cleanup guidance and passes typecheck, full test, repo doctor, and dist smoke validation
description: Improve ai-harness doctor so it can spot deprecated or conflicting workflow artifacts that should not survive alongside the harness foundation. The doctor output should help a user distinguish between missing required harness files, outdated known leftovers, and safe user-owned files that should be preserved.
title: Teach doctor to flag deprecated AI workflow artifacts

acceptance_criteria: The self-hosted repo and sample adoption targets validate cleanly, with any newly discovered work filed as linked follow-ups before Phase 2 closes.
close_reason: Verified: self-hosted dry-run adoption, repo doctor, sample existing-repo adoption, full tests, and dist smoke all passed with no new follow-up issues required
description: Run the harness flow end to end on this repository and on one or more sample repos once doctrine, cleanup, fixtures, and doctor guidance have landed. Capture gaps as follow-up issues instead of hiding them, and use this task to decide when Phase 2 is truly verified.
title: Validate the dogfood loop on self-hosted and sample repos

close_reason: Verified autonomous workflow scaffold at .codex/workflows/autonomous-execution.md, aligned runtime docs, and chose not to hard-code Beads export config until version-specific keys are validated; confirmed with pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:smoke:dist, and ai-harness doctor . --assistant codex
description: Follow up on the remaining gap between the desired autonomous GSD workflow integration and the current ai-harness scaffold. The repo currently has no autonomous.md surface, and the proposed Beads config key export_to_jsonl does not match the active upstream .beads/config.yaml comments in this environment, which reference events-export instead. Decide whether ai-harness should scaffold a dedicated autonomous workflow doc, how that should coexist with AGENTS.md and .codex docs, and whether post-bd-init config should enable JSONL or event export for automation.
title: Decide autonomous.md and Beads config integration strategy

close_reason: Verified Beads/GSD harmonization docs and scripts with pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:smoke:dist, and ai-harness doctor . --assistant codex
description: Audit and tighten ai-harness scaffold docs, runtime scripts, and rules so the documented workflow clearly follows Beads -> GSD -> Beads. Cover graceful degradation when bd or .beads is unavailable, phase-level issue tracking expectations, verification-before-close rules, and landing/bootstrap guidance. Note the current gap that no autonomous.md exists in this repo and avoid hard-coding unsupported Beads config keys without version validation.
title: Harmonize Beads and GSD workflow guidance

acceptance_criteria: Phase 3 has clear child tasks, the distribution direction is explicit, and downstream migration/versioning work is ready to execute after Phase 2 closes.
description: Finish CORE-06 by deciding how ai-harness is installed and updated beyond a local checkout, defining the downstream versioning and upgrade story, and documenting migration from older scaiff-era or legacy workflow setups.
title: Phase 3: distribution and migration readiness

acceptance_criteria: The chosen distribution path is explicit enough to drive packaging, install docs, and follow-up implementation work.
close_reason: Decided on a staged hybrid distribution model: support local-source installation now, defer package-manager publication until versioning and migration guidance are explicit, and documented the decision in README.md and .planning/phases/phase-3-distribution-readiness/RESEARCH.md
description: Choose how ai-harness should be installed and updated outside this checkout. Compare the realistic options such as local-source install only, package manager distribution, or a staged hybrid approach, and pick the path that fits the current product and support burden.
title: Decide the ai-harness distribution model

acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete versioning and upgrade story that downstream repos can follow without guessing which scaffold behavior they are on.
close_reason: Verified: downstream baseline/version guidance landed and Phase 3 verification passed (.planning/phases/phase-3-distribution-readiness/VERIFICATION.md)
description: Decide how downstream repositories should understand which ai-harness generation they were scaffolded from and how to upgrade safely over time. Cover template/source/dist drift expectations, compatibility boundaries, and what an upgrade path should promise for existing repos.
title: Define downstream versioning and upgrade guidance

acceptance_criteria: The compatibility stance for older scaiff references is documented clearly enough to support install and migration messaging.
close_reason: Verified: scaiff compatibility stance and no-alias policy are documented and Phase 3 verification passed (.planning/phases/phase-3-distribution-readiness/VERIFICATION.md)
description: Decide whether older scaiff-era users get a temporary alias, a migration-only doc path, or no compatibility layer beyond documentation. Make the answer explicit so distribution and migration work do not stall on an unresolved naming question.
title: Document scaiff compatibility and alias strategy

acceptance_criteria: Install and update guidance is ready to ship once the Phase 3 decisions land, with no major open questions left in the downstream operator path.
close_reason: Verified: downstream install/update guidance shipped and Phase 3 verification passed (.planning/phases/phase-3-distribution-readiness/VERIFICATION.md)
description: Once distribution, versioning, and compatibility decisions are made, turn them into concrete downstream guidance for new installs and upgrades. The output should tell other repos how to adopt ai-harness, update it later, and understand any migration caveats.
title: Publish install and update guidance for downstream repos

close_reason: Verified: aligned docs, templates, and Phase 3 artifacts to an explicit local-source-only distribution model; pnpm typecheck, targeted integration tests, and pnpm test:smoke:dist passed
description: Update repo docs, planning artifacts, and phase artifacts so ai-harness is clearly documented as a local-source-only tool with no future package publication path implied. Replace publish-readiness and staged-hybrid wording with an explicit local-only stance, and keep the scaiff no-alias policy intact.
title: Align local-only distribution stance

close_reason: Cleaned the repo up for project-local Beads usage only: removed Dolt remote sync guidance from landing docs/scripts, stopped requiring BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD in scaffolded env files and doctor checks, and verified local-only downstream adoption with pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:smoke:dist, and ai-harness doctor.
description: The updated landing workflow now prefers 'bd dolt pull' and 'bd dolt push', but this repository currently has no configured Dolt remote. Running 'bd dolt pull' fails with 'remote origin not found'. Decide whether ai-harness should document this as optional local setup, add clearer graceful degradation in landing guidance, or configure a real Dolt remote for the shared backlog.
title: Configure Beads Dolt remote for landing workflow

close_reason: Removed transient Beads monitor files from version control and ignored them in .gitignore so local bd activity no longer dirties the worktree.
description: Stop tracking transient .beads runtime files such as dolt-monitor.pid and dolt-server.activity so the ai-harness worktree can stay clean after running bd commands.
title: Ignore Beads runtime monitor files

description: Carry the local-source-only distribution stance into the CLI and runtime messaging so help text, init/install-skill output, and doctor guidance all reinforce the supported checkout + install:local flow.
title: Add local-only runtime messaging

close_reason: Verified: pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:smoke:dist, and ai-harness doctor passed
description: Finish the Phase 2 project-local Beads cleanup by removing the stale BEADS_DOLT_PASSWORD placeholder from fresh .env.example scaffolds and fixing duplicate doctor warnings/groups introduced during the same pass.
title: Align project-local Beads scaffold outputs

close_reason: Replaced deprecated bd sync guidance with bd dolt pull/push in landing docs and scripts; verified with pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, and pnpm test:smoke:dist.
description: Update ai-harness docs, runtime scripts, and scaffold templates to stop recommending deprecated 'bd sync' usage. Prefer the current 'bd dolt pull' and 'bd dolt push' flow where appropriate, and verify downstream scaffold snapshots stay aligned.
title: Replace deprecated bd sync guidance
